title: America's Aluminum Supply Hits Zero in 30 Days
summary: The United States faces a severe aluminum supply crisis, with Bloomberg warning that supplies could hit zero within 30 days due to a 50% tariff on Canadian aluminum disrupting imports. Production disruptions in the Middle East have eliminated major foreign sources, threatening the manufacturing base. Bank of America warns the deficit could widen to 1.5 million tons under prolonged conditions.

title: Aluminum Hits Near 4-Year High
summary: Aluminum futures in the UK rose to $3,270 per tonne, the highest since April 2022, driven by tightening global supply amid rising demand from the energy transition. China's output hit a record 45.02 million tons in 2025, reaching its capacity cap, while disruptions at smelters in Iceland, Mozambique, and Australia limit availability. A weaker US dollar further supported prices.

title: Goodbye to critical metals? Scientists create a new revolutionary aluminum that could slash industrial costs, accelerate the energy transition
summary: Scientists have developed a new aluminum with properties that redefine production processes and reduce dependence on expensive, limited critical metals. This innovation lowers industrial costs, improves supply chain predictability, and accelerates the energy transition toward sustainable clean technologies. It marks a turning point for more efficient and accessible production in industry.

title: Ball Corporation reports record 2025 results as aluminium sustainability metrics advance
summary: Ball Corporation published its 2025 Combined Annual and Sustainability Report, highlighting record financial results with $13.16 billion in net sales from 111.9 billion units of aluminum packaging. Sustainability advanced with 74% of aluminum from recycled sources and 84% of production facilities powered by renewable energy. This progress supports ongoing targets in aluminum sustainability for beverage packaging.

title: United States Steel Corporation announced a US$1.9 billion investment to construct a new direct reduced iron (DRI) facility at Big River Steel
summary: United States Steel Corporation has announced a major US$1.9 billion investment to build a new direct reduced iron (DRI) facility at its Big River Steel operations. This expansion aims to boost steel production capacity through advanced DRI technology. The project represents a significant step in enhancing domestic steelmaking capabilities.

title: Argentina's crude steel output increases 42.3 percent in March, amid erratic demand
summary: Argentina's crude steel production surged by 42.3 percent in March despite facing erratic demand conditions. This notable increase highlights resilience in the local steel sector. The data reflects recent developments in Latin American steel output.

title: United States Steel Corporation announced a US$1.9 billion investment to construct a new direct reduced iron (DRI) facility at Big River Steel
summary: United States Steel Corporation announced a US$1.9 billion investment to build a new direct reduced iron (DRI) facility at Big River Steel Works in Osceola, Arkansas, marking the first of its kind in the U.S. This initiative advances sustainable steel production by utilizing DRI technology, which supports lower emissions compared to traditional methods. The project aligns with innovations in clean iron production for enhanced environmental performance.

title: POSCO and Electra Team Up, Invest in Clean Iron Production
summary: Clean iron technology company Electra signed a joint development agreement with POSCO to scale clean iron production technologies, accompanied by an investment from POSCO. This partnership focuses on innovative processes to reduce emissions in steelmaking. It represents a key step in sustainable steel technology development.

title: ArcelorMittal Teams Up With Stockland for Green Steel
summary: ArcelorMittal International, Steligence®, and partner JSteel collaborated with Stockland to deliver engineered solutions for green steel projects. The partnership emphasizes tailor-made sustainable steel applications. This initiative promotes sustainability in construction through innovative steel technologies.

title: Copper futures steadied around $5.9 per pound after five consecutive sessions of losses
summary: Copper fell to 5.91 USD/Lbs on May 1, 2026, down 0.20% from the previous day. Industrial metals drew support from stronger-than-expected expansion in China's manufacturing activity despite ongoing pressure from the Middle East conflict.

title: Chilean miner Codelco's El Teniente smelter to restart after collapse
summary: Chilean miner Codelco announced that its smelter at the El Teniente mine is set to restart on Thursday following a collapse. This development could impact copper supply dynamics in the market.

title: Copper rebounds toward $13000 on supply squeeze
summary: Copper prices have staged a sharp recovery to around $13,000 per metric ton after briefly falling below $12,000/mt, driven by mine disruptions and a renewed risk-on tone in global markets. Chile reported an 8.4% month-on-month drop in February copper output to 375,400 metric tons, while Peru's production declined 0.9% to 201,414 metric tons. The rebound highlights ongoing supply squeeze pressures in the copper market.

title: US imposes sanctions on Congo armed group, mining firms over illicit minerals
summary: The United States announced sanctions against an armed group aligned with the DRC government and associated mining firms involved in illicit minerals trade. This action targets disruptions in the copper-rich Democratic Republic of Congo, a key global copper producer. The sanctions aim to curb illegal mining activities impacting legitimate copper production.

title: Technology Minerals unlocks new innovation and revenue stream with copper and aluminium recovery
summary: Technology Minerals has introduced a new innovation for recovering copper and aluminium from batteries, creating a novel revenue stream. This technology supports sustainability by enhancing recycling efforts in the battery sector. It aligns with growing demands for sustainable metal recovery amid rising copper needs.

title: Copper's quiet supply squeeze is reshaping the investment case
summary: Copper is experiencing a structural supply deficit due to electrification, AI demands, and declining ore grades. Electric vehicles require significantly more copper, and global demand is projected to rise 50% by 2040. This supply bottleneck is decades in the making while demand accelerates across industries.

title: Innovations to close the copper supply-demand gap
summary: A looming copper supply-demand gap requires new technologies and policies to bridge it, especially as ore grades decline. Innovations are essential for sustainable production to meet demands from green technologies. Copper's critical role in electrification underscores the urgency of these advancements.

title: Nickel - Price - Chart - Historical Data - News
summary: Nickel rose to 19,410 USD/T on May 1, 2026, up 0.10% from the previous day, with a 13.51% increase over the past month and 25.79% year-over-year. Futures traded above $19,400, a near two-year high, driven by supply disruptions including mining quota cuts in Indonesia and a sulfur shortage from Strait of Hormuz disruptions. These factors have constrained ore supply and raised processing costs for nickel intermediates.

title: Live Nickel Prices Today • Nickel Spot and Futures Price
summary: Nickel prices advanced by 0.64%, settling at $19,364.63/Ton globally and ¥132,269/Ton in China, driven by supply constraints. Indonesia slashed mining quotas, tightening ore availability, while a global sulfur shortage from Middle East tensions threatens MHP processing. A weaker US dollar and firm SHFE quotes further support the bullish momentum for this battery metal.
